title: quercus humilis subsp. subpyrenaica
intro: Quercus humilis subsp. subpyrenaica, also known as the Pyrenean dwarf oak, is a subspecies of the Quercus humilis, a small deciduous shrub native to the Pyrenees mountains. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of various polyphenolic compounds. The health benefits of Quercus humilis subsp. subpyrenaica include its ability to protect against oxidative stress, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Quercus humilis subsp. subpyrenaica can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are often used in traditional medicine in the form of tinctures or extracts.
